summary: Implemented scheduled D1 backup worker with R2 storage. Standalone Cloudflare Worker with daily cron trigger, gzip compression, 90-day retention, manual trigger endpoint, and GitHub Actions deployment workflow. PR #16 created.
output:
Platform: node-cloudflare. No Jira integration (not configured). Implementation based on design notes at implementation-design/crm-d1-backup/implementation-notes.md.

Created files:
- projects/crm-backup/wrangler.toml — Worker config with cron trigger (daily 02:00 UTC), R2 binding, D1 database ID, 90-day retention
- projects/crm-backup/package.json — Minimal package with wrangler dev dependency
- projects/crm-backup/src/index.js — Full backup logic: D1 export API with polling, gzip compression via CompressionStream, R2 storage with metadata, verification, retention-based pruning, manual HTTP trigger with Bearer auth, health check endpoint
- .github/workflows/deploy-crm-backup.yml — GitHub Actions workflow triggered on push to main with path filter projects/crm-backup/**

Dry-run deploy passed: 4.78 KiB / gzip 1.83 KiB. All bindings verified.

Branch: feature/crm-d1-backup
Commit: 078e55c
PR: https://github.com/Lintlinger/CloudRise/pull/16

Manual steps required before first deploy:
1. Create R2 bucket: wrangler r2 bucket create crm-backups
2. Deploy worker: cd projects/crm-backup && wrangler deploy
3. Set secrets: wrangler secret put CF_API_TOKEN, CF_ACCOUNT_ID, BACKUP_SECRET
4. Verify cron in Cloudflare dashboard
